name: iteratively reweighted least squares
description: An iterative algorithm (IRLS) for fitting generalized linear models like logistic regression, where each iteration solves a weighted least squares problem with data-dependent weights.

name: adjusted response in logistic regression
description: The linearized response z = Xβ + W^(-1)(y − p) used in the IRLS algorithm for logistic regression, representing a working response.

name: wald test
description: A hypothesis test for whether a coefficient is zero based on the ratio of the estimate to its standard error (Z score), used for variable selection in logistic regression.

name: residual deviance
description: Minus twice the log-likelihood of a fitted model, used to measure model fit and compare nested models in generalized linear models.

name: pearson chi-square statistic
description: A goodness-of-fit statistic equal to the weighted residual sum-of-squares in logistic regression, measuring the discrepancy between observed and fitted values.

name: rao score test
description: A hypothesis test for including a term in a model based on the score function at the current parameter estimates, not requiring iterative fitting.

name: l1 regularized logistic regression
description: Logistic regression with an L1 penalty on the coefficients, promoting sparsity and automatic variable selection.

name: conditional likelihood
description: The likelihood of the response given the predictors, Pr(G|X), used in logistic regression which ignores the marginal distribution of X.

name: joint likelihood in lda
description: The full likelihood Pr(X,G) = Pr(X)Pr(G|X) used in LDA, incorporating both the marginal distribution of X and the class posteriors.

name: separating hyperplane
description: A hyperplane that perfectly separates two classes in feature space, with all points of one class on one side and all points of the other class on the opposite side.

name: perceptron
description: A linear classifier that computes a linear combination of input features and returns the sign, developed by Rosenblatt in the 1950s as a foundation for neural networks.

name: affine set
description: A set defined by a linear equation f(x) = β₀ + β^T x = 0, generalizing hyperplanes to not necessarily pass through the origin.

name: signed distance to hyperplane
description: The perpendicular distance from a point to a hyperplane, with sign indicating which side of the hyperplane the point lies on, given by f(x)/||β||.

name: perceptron learning algorithm
description: An algorithm that finds a separating hyperplane by minimizing the distance of misclassified points to the decision boundary using stochastic gradient descent.

name: stochastic gradient descent
description: An optimization method that updates parameters after each observation rather than computing gradients over the entire dataset, used in the perceptron algorithm.

name: optimal separating hyperplane
description: The hyperplane that separates two classes while maximizing the margin (minimum distance) to the closest point from either class, providing a unique solution.

name: margin in classification
description: The product yf(x) in classification with y ∈ {-1,1}. Positive margin indicates correct classification, negative margin indicates misclassification. Analogous to residuals in regression.

name: lagrange primal function
description: The objective function augmented with Lagrange multipliers for constraints, minimized to solve constrained optimization problems.

name: wolfe dual
description: The dual formulation of a constrained optimization problem, obtained by substituting optimality conditions into the Lagrangian, often simpler to solve.

name: karush-kuhn-tucker conditions
description: Necessary and sufficient conditions for optimality in constrained optimization, including the primal-dual relationships and complementarity slackness.

name: support points in separating hyperplanes
description: Training points that lie exactly on the margin boundary (where αᵢ > 0), which define the solution vector β in the optimal separating hyperplane.

name: basis expansion
description: A technique of augmenting or replacing input variables X with transformations hm(X) to create a richer feature space, then fitting linear models in this expanded space.

name: piecewise polynomial
description: A function obtained by dividing the domain into contiguous intervals and representing the function by a separate polynomial in each interval.

name: knots in splines
description: The boundary points ξ₁, ξ₂, ... where the piecewise polynomial segments join, defining the intervals in piecewise polynomial regression.

name: continuity constraints in splines
description: Linear constraints on parameters ensuring that piecewise polynomial segments connect smoothly at knots, reducing the effective number of parameters.

name: truncated power basis
description: A basis for representing continuous piecewise linear functions using terms like (x − ξ)₊ that are zero below the knot and linear above.

name: natural cubic spline
description: A cubic spline with the additional constraint that the function is linear beyond the boundary knots, reducing boundary variance.

name: order-M spline
description: A piecewise-polynomial of order M with continuous derivatives up to order M-2 at the knots.

name: cubic spline
description: An order-4 spline with continuous first and second derivatives at knots, the lowest order where knot discontinuity is invisible to the human eye.

name: pointwise variance
description: The variance of a fitted function at a specific point, measuring uncertainty in the fit at that location.

name: backward stepwise deletion
description: A model selection procedure that iteratively removes terms from a model to minimize an information criterion while preserving group structure.

name: functional modeling
description: Using splines to reduce rather than increase flexibility by constraining coefficients to vary smoothly as functions.

name: contrast functional
description: A function of coefficients that highlights regions where classes differ, having appreciable values where features discriminate between classes.

name: filtered features
description: Transformed features x* derived from raw features x through basis expansion or other preprocessing.

name: smoothing spline
description: A spline that minimizes penalized RSS with a knot at every unique x value, controlling complexity via a roughness penalty rather than knot selection.

name: penalized residual sum of squares
description: An objective function balancing data fit against function roughness, with a smoothing parameter controlling the tradeoff.

name: Sobolev space
description: An infinite-dimensional function space of functions with bounded derivatives, used as the domain for smoothing spline optimization.

name: generalized ridge regression
description: Ridge regression with a general penalty matrix rather than identity, arising from smoothing spline formulation.

name: Reinsch form
description: An alternative representation Sλ = (I + λK)⁻¹ separating the smoothing parameter from the penalty structure.

name: projection smoothers
description: Smoothers with idempotent smoother matrices having eigenvalues of exactly 1 or 0, selecting basis functions without shrinkage.

name: Demmler-Reinsch basis
description: The eigenvector basis of the smoother matrix, providing a representation where the penalty is diagonal.

name: integrated squared prediction error
description: Expected prediction error averaged over both training samples and prediction points, combining bias and variance.

name: nonparametric logistic regression
description: Logistic regression with a smooth nonparametric function replacing the linear predictor, fit via penalized log-likelihood.

name: penalized log-likelihood
description: A log-likelihood criterion augmented with a roughness penalty for fitting smooth nonparametric models.

name: weighted smoothing spline
description: A smoothing spline fit with observation-specific weights, arising in iterative algorithms like Newton-Raphson for GLMs.

name: working response
description: In IRLS algorithms, an adjusted response variable computed at each iteration for fitting weighted regression.

name: tensor product basis
description: A multidimensional basis formed by taking products of one-dimensional basis functions for each coordinate.

name: reproducing kernel hilbert space
description: A mathematical framework (RKHS) for function estimation with kernels, providing theoretical foundation for support vector machines.

name: wavelet smoothing
description: A smoothing method using wavelet basis functions, effective for functions with discontinuities and sharp features.

name: wavelet basis
description: A basis of wavelet functions for representing signals, allowing multi-resolution analysis.

name: wavelet transform
description: A transformation decomposing signals into wavelet coefficients at different scales and locations.

name: adaptive wavelet filtering
description: A wavelet smoothing method that adaptively selects coefficients based on data-driven criteria.

name: kernel smoother
description: A nonparametric regression method using kernel-weighted local averages.

name: AIC statistic
description: Akaike Information Criterion - a model selection criterion balancing fit and complexity, used for term selection in stepwise procedures.

name: B-spline basis
description: A numerically stable basis for representing splines, allowing efficient computation even with many knots.

name: boundary knots
description: The extreme knots defining the domain boundaries where spline behavior can become erratic.

name: interior knots
description: Knots placed within the data range, controlling spline flexibility in regions with data.

name: generalized cross-validation
description: A convenient approximation to leave-one-out cross-validation for linear fitting under squared-error loss, using GCV = (1/N)Σᵢ[yᵢ - f̂(xᵢ)]²/(1 - trace(S)/N)² instead of computing individual diagonal elements Sᵢᵢ.

name: cp statistic
description: An estimate of expected prediction error for linear models under squared error loss, equal to training error plus (2d/N)σ̂²_ε where d is the number of parameters, adjusting for optimism.

name: automatic kernel carpentry
description: The phenomenon whereby local linear regression automatically modifies the kernel to correct the bias exactly to first order, without requiring manual kernel modifications based on asymptotic theory.

name: idempotent matrix
description: A matrix A satisfying A² = A, characteristic of projection operators in linear algebra.

name: positive semidefinite matrix
description: A symmetric matrix with non-negative eigenvalues, ensuring non-negative quadratic forms.

name: eigendecomposition
description: The decomposition of a matrix into eigenvectors and eigenvalues, revealing the structure of linear operators.

name: MARS
description: Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines - a greedy algorithm for adaptive knot placement in regression splines.

name: local polynomial regression
description: An extension of local linear regression that fits polynomials of degree d locally, providing bias reduction at the cost of increased variance. Local quadratic regression (d=2) can correct curvature bias in the interior of the domain.

name: trimming the hills and filling the valleys
description: A bias phenomenon in local linear fits where the estimator tends to be biased in regions of curvature of the true function, underestimating peaks and overestimating valleys.

name: nadaraya-watson kernel smoother
description: A kernel smoothing method that fits a constant locally with weights supplied by a kernel, which suffers from boundary bias that can be corrected by local linear regression.

name: varying coefficient models
description: A class of structured regression models where the model is conditionally linear for some predictors, but the coefficients are allowed to vary smoothly as a function of other covariates, fit using locally weighted least squares.

name: local likelihood
description: A method that generalizes local regression by using likelihood-based models fit locally, allowing parametric models to be made locally flexible. Any parametric model can be made local if the fitting method accommodates observation weights.

name: parzen density estimate
description: A smooth kernel density estimator that counts observations close to a point with weights that decrease with distance, using a kernel such as the Gaussian. It is the convolution of the empirical distribution with a smoothing kernel.

name: naive bayes classifier
description: A classification method that assumes features are independent given the class, dramatically simplifying density estimation by estimating only univariate marginal densities. Despite strong assumptions, it often outperforms more sophisticated alternatives.

name: radial basis functions
description: A method that treats kernel functions as basis functions indexed by location parameters ξⱼ and scale parameters λⱼ, combining ideas from kernel methods and basis function expansions to model f(x) = Σⱼ βⱼ D(||x-ξⱼ||/λⱼ).

name: rbf network
description: A neural network alternative where all parameters (locations ξⱼ, scales λⱼ, and coefficients βⱼ) are optimized jointly via sum-of-squares minimization, though the criterion is nonconvex with multiple local minima.

name: renormalized radial basis functions
description: A modification of radial basis functions that normalizes each basis function by the sum of all basis functions, avoiding holes (regions where no kernel has appreciable support) when using constant scale parameters.

name: gaussian mixture model
description: A density estimation method representing a distribution as a weighted sum of Gaussian densities with mixing proportions αₘ, means μₘ, and covariance matrices Σₘ, typically fit by maximum likelihood using the EM algorithm.

name: memory-based methods
description: Learning methods where the model is the entire training data set and fitting is done at evaluation or prediction time, leading to O(N) computational cost per evaluation but making them infeasible for real-time applications.

name: model selection
description: The task of estimating the performance of different models in order to choose the best one, typically by estimating prediction error for various tuning parameters.

name: model assessment
description: The task of estimating the prediction error (generalization error) of a final chosen model on new data, ideally using a held-out test set kept in a vault.

name: model bias
description: The error between the best-fitting model in a restricted class and the true function, representing limitations of the model family itself rather than estimation error.

name: estimation bias
description: The error between the average estimate and the best-fitting model within a class. Zero for ordinary least squares but positive for restricted fits like ridge regression.

name: optimism
description: The difference between in-sample error and training error, representing how much training error underestimates true error. For many loss functions, optimism = (2/N)Σᵢ Cov(ŷᵢ, yᵢ).

name: akaike information criterion
description: A general estimate of expected prediction error for log-likelihood loss, using the formula -2·loglik + 2·d, relying on the asymptotic relationship ω = (2/N)Σᵢ Cov(ŷᵢ, yᵢ).

name: effective number of parameters
description: For linear fitting methods ŷ = Sy, defined as trace(S), generalizing the concept of number of parameters to regularized models. Replaces d in the Cₚ statistic for non-standard fits.

name: bayesian information criterion
description: A model selection criterion with the form -2·loglik + log(N)·d, also known as the Schwarz criterion. Motivated by Bayesian posterior probability and equivalent to choosing the model with largest approximate posterior probability.

name: laplace approximation
description: An approximation technique used to approximate the integral Pr(Z|Mₘ) in Bayesian model comparison, leading to the BIC criterion after simplifications.

name: asymptotic consistency
description: A property of a model selection criterion whereby the probability of selecting the correct model approaches one as sample size N→∞. BIC is asymptotically consistent while AIC is not.

name: minimum description length
description: A model selection approach formally identical to BIC but motivated from optimal coding and data compression theory. The principle states that one should choose the model minimizing the total message length for transmitting both parameters and data.

name: shannon entropy
description: A measure of information content in a distribution, equal to -Σᵢ Pr(zᵢ)log₂ Pr(zᵢ) for discrete distributions. Optimal code lengths achieve this lower bound when probabilities satisfy pᵢ = A^(-lᵢ).

name: vapnik-chervonenkis dimension
description: A measure of model complexity defined as the largest number of points (in some configuration) that can be shattered by members of a function class. A set of points is shattered if the class can perfectly separate any binary labeling of them.

name: structural risk minimization
description: Vapnik's approach to model selection that fits a nested sequence of models of increasing VC dimensions and chooses the model minimizing an upper bound on test error based on VC theory.

name: k-fold cross-validation
description: A method for estimating prediction error by splitting data into K parts, fitting the model to K-1 parts and testing on the remaining part, then averaging the K prediction errors. Typical choices are K=5 or K=10.

name: one-standard-error rule
description: A model selection heuristic used with cross-validation that chooses the most parsimonious model whose error is no more than one standard error above the error of the best model.

name: epanechnikov kernel
description: A compact support kernel function used in local regression, flatter on top like nearest-neighbor box kernels and requiring careful choice of window size.

name: tri-cube kernel
description: A popular compact support kernel function that is flatter on top than the Epanechnikov kernel and is differentiable at the boundary of its support, used in local regression.

name: boundary bias in kernel methods
description: A bias problem in kernel smoothing where locally-weighted averages are badly biased on the boundaries of the domain because of kernel asymmetry. Local linear regression corrects this bias exactly to first order.

name: curse of dimensionality in local regression
description: The phenomenon that in high dimensions, it becomes impossible to simultaneously maintain localness (low bias) and a sizable neighborhood sample (low variance) without exponential sample size growth. The fraction of boundary points approaches one as dimension grows.
